description:
You are assisting a compliance-officer who is performing a very small certificate-expiration spot-check on one Linux host.

Current filesystem layout (already present when the shell session starts)

  /home/user/certs/
      ├── website_valid.pem
      └── old_service.pem

Each “*.pem” file is a plain text stub that contains a single line with the string

    notAfter=<RFC-2822-style-date>

Example:
    notAfter=Nov 12 12:00:00 2031 GMT

Assignment
1. Read the notAfter value from every “*.pem” file in /home/user/certs.
2. Compare the timestamp against the fixed audit cutoff “2025-01-01 00:00:00 GMT”.
   • If the certificate expires strictly after that cutoff, mark it “VALID”.
   • Otherwise mark it “EXPIRED”.
3. Produce the report file /home/user/certs/compliance_audit.log.

STRICT output format for /home/user/certs/compliance_audit.log
• It is plain text.
• One line per certificate, no blank lines, **exactly** this pipe-separated layout:

    <filename>|<notAfter-value>|<STATUS>

    where
       <filename>      = basename of the processed certificate file
       <notAfter-value>= the string that appears after “notAfter=” in the file
       <STATUS>        = either the literal word “VALID” or “EXPIRED”

• Lines may appear in any order, but each line must end with a single newline character.

Example of a syntactically correct report line
    website_valid.pem|Nov 12 12:00:00 2031 GMT|VALID

No additional commentary, headers or timestamps are allowed inside the log file.

Hints for the agent (do NOT reveal commands):
• Use common shell utilities (grep, awk, cut, etc.) to extract the notAfter value.
• Convert the fixed cutoff string into something that can be compared, or use string comparison if easier—whichever you prefer, as long as the final verdict is correct.
• Verify the final file by displaying it in the terminal before finishing.

Success criteria checked by the automated grader
• /home/user/certs/compliance_audit.log exists.
• The file is world-readable.
• It contains exactly two non-empty lines, each matching the format shown above.
• The line for “website_valid.pem” ends with “|VALID”.
• The line for “old_service.pem” ends with “|EXPIRED”.

You do NOT need root privileges. A total of three to four commands is sufficient.
truth:
Initial files present before the task starts:

1) /home/user/certs/website_valid.pem
   Contents (including terminating newline)
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
notAfter=Nov 12 12:00:00 2031 GMT

2) /home/user/certs/old_service.pem
   Contents (including terminating newline)
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
notAfter=Jan  1 00:00:00 2021 GMT

Expected file created by the agent:

/home/user/certs/compliance_audit.log
Required exact content (two lines, each ending with newline, order irrelevant):

website_valid.pem|Nov 12 12:00:00 2031 GMT|VALID
old_service.pem|Jan  1 00:00:00 2021 GMT|EXPIRED

(If the agent chooses the opposite order that is also accepted, as grading logic sorts the lines.)
Permissions: rw-r--r-- (0644) or more permissive.
